name: The White House
subject: Vice President Pence makes an important pit stop
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=af5c2386552c682a33599f54ad367ea602d3e528d9c20b8ac1f1f83377d8104abae4f5d13e70a7b3b22d9cd562b2c29ddeacda0b2619c8d7

Vice President Pence makes an important pit stop

Vice President Mike Pence arrived in Japan yesterday to begin meeting with foreign leaders and building on the momentum of President Donald J. Trump’s historic visit to Asia last year.

But before the Vice President flew to Japan, he made a special stop in Alaska at Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson on Monday evening. Gov. Bill Walker (I-AK) was at the Vice President’s side.

“As I prepare to travel to Japan and South Korea, I thought it was altogether fitting to begin here at Elmendorf, the first line of defense for the American people,” the Vice President said. “Missile defense begins here in Alaska. And the American people and the world should know that our nation is secure.

“Our nation's defenses from potential inbound missile attacks is the best in the world.”

Read more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=af5c2386552c682aa16cd699de0e4c04042616225048d1f4db2512fffcf1578670a60276a8eebce3dff21f786bbe7a3c50a08aa1b1af3004
Vice President Mike Pence arrives in Japan

‘We can only be as good as the law’

President Trump hosted law enforcement professionals, community leaders, and several members of Congress yesterday for a roundtable at the White House on how best to confront the scourge of gang violence from MS-13.

“We’re doing much better than any administration by far,” the President said. “Still, we have a long way to go. We can only be as good as the law.” He pressed lawmakers on the urgency of addressing border security problems as part of comprehensive immigration reform in the coming weeks.

“So, we have one good shot at it, and it's now."

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=af5c2386552c682a9954982685e81fed3afd06238f395fd20935bc3c43693f9b2321acdb3aeb66bf157351e53fe54e16abe41b5e65dee0e5
Read more about how President Trump plans to protect American communities from MS-13.

140th White House Easter Egg Roll

First Lady Melania Trump announced yesterday that the White House is excited to host its 140th annual Easter Egg Roll on Monday, April 2, on the White House South Lawn.

The details are as follows:

- Families with children under 13 are invited to attend and enjoy egg rolls, live entertainment, and sports.
- Tickets are free to the public and can be requested through an online lottery that opens this Friday, February 9, at noon ET. The lottery will close on Thursday, February 15, at noon ET. Winners will be notified via email no later than Monday, March 5.
- Volunteers will be selected through an online application process. The deadline to apply is February 23 at noon ET.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=af5c2386552c682a978d606be201ce60e26c7d8b57efe25efcd80ca8365c9d0041d050ca5f00b9dec1e77a77c9e4f5f0311c368227cd0a14
See more on the history of the 140-year-old White House Easter Egg Roll.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump participates in a roundtable discussion concerning MS-13 gang activity | February 6, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will meet with the Secretary of Health and Human Services. In the afternoon, he will meet with the Chair of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission and then with Republican Members of the Senate Finance Committee.

In the evening, the President will host the National Prayer Breakfast Dinner.

Bill Walker (I-AK) was at the Vice President’s side. “As I prepare to travel to Japan and South Korea, I thought it was altogether fitting to begin here at Elmendorf, the first line of defense for the American people,” the Vice President said. “Missile defense begins here in Alaska. And the American people and the world should know that our nation is secure. “Our nation's defenses from potential inbound missile attacks is the best in the world.” Read more: Vice President Mike Pence arrives in Japan ‘We can only be as good as the law’ President Trump hosted law enforcement professionals, community leaders, and several members of Congress yesterday for a roundtable at the White House on how best to confront the scourge of gang violence from MS-13. “We’re doing much better than any administration by far,” the President said. “Still, we have a long way to go. We can only be as good as the law.” He pressed lawmakers on the urgency of addressing border security problems as part of comprehensive immigration reform in the coming weeks. “So, we have one good shot at it, and it's now." Read more about how President Trump plans to protect American communities from MS-13. 140th White House Easter Egg Roll First Lady Melania Trump announced yesterday that the White House is excited to host its 140th annual Easter Egg Roll on Monday, April 2, on the White House South Lawn. The details are as follows: - Families with children under 13 are invited to attend and enjoy egg rolls, live entertainment, and sports. - Tickets are free to the public and can be requested through an online lottery that opens this Friday, February 9, at noon ET. The lottery will close on Thursday, February 15, at noon ET. Winners will be notified via email no later than Monday, March 5. - Volunteers will be selected through an online application process. The deadline to apply is February 23 at noon ET. See more on the history of the 140-year-old White House Easter Egg Roll. Photo of the Day President Donald J. Trump participates in a roundtable discussion concerning MS-13 gang activity | February 6, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian) POTUS TODAY This morning, President Trump will meet with the Secretary of Health and Human Services. In the afternoon, he will meet with the Chair of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission and then with Republican Members of the Senate Finance Committee. In the evening, the President will host the National Prayer Breakfast Dinner.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Henry_R=2E_Mu=C3=B1oz_III=2C_Democrats=2Eorg?=
subject: The truth about DNC fundraising:
body:
We can win when the Republicans outspend us, but we need to keep it close.
---
Derek --

I'm Henry Muñoz, the Finance Chair for the Democratic Party. Given my role here, it's probably not a surprise to you that I'm writing to talk about money, and where the DNC stands. I think it's crucial that we take a moment to assess where we are.

In 2017, the Democratic National Committee raised $65.9 million.

That's a lot of money. It's more than any off-cycle year since 2011, and more than we raised in 2005 and 2007 -- years that preceded the Democratic takeover of both chambers of Congress and the election of Barack Obama, respectively.

How are we doing this? It's not because millionaires and billionaires are donating at historic levels -- it's from lots of grassroots donors chipping in what they can, when they can. Nearly two-thirds of the money we raised in 2017 came from supporters giving $200 or less, with an average online donation of $21. For any organization, that's a remarkable number -- and it shows just how excited people are to elect Democrats.

If we're going to win this year, we need supporters like you to take that excitement and energy to the next level. The Democratic Party is going to go as far as you take it, and not an inch further, Derek.

**Chip in $3 if you're ready to elect Democrats everywhere this year, and let's keep building this party.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223f4/1181813120/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/support-the-dnc?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223f5/1181813120/VEsEAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/support-the-dnc?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=26+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223f2/1181813120/VEsEAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/support-the-dnc?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=27+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223f3/1181813120/VEsEDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/support-the-dnc?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=28+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223f0/1181813120/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/support-the-dnc?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223f1/1181813120/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-the-dnc?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")
** If you're still wondering about the power of people like you who give $15 or $25 to impact elections, look no further than last year's marquee races.

Grassroots donors helped the DNC invest $1.5 million to elect Ralph Northam as governor of Virginia. Democrats came together to put him in a strong position to actually govern by flipping 15 seats from red to blue in the House of Delegates -- a feat the state hasn't seen since the late 1800s.

In Alabama, the RNC cut big checks at Donald Trump's request in a desperate attempt to bail out an alleged child molester. We took a different approach (to say the least), and rode a wave of grassroots enthusiasm powered by over 40,000 contributors. The result? The DNC was able to support Doug Jones's campaign to the tune of nearly $1 million, including almost $700,000 that came from supporters who split a donation between the DNC and Doug Jones campaign. That money funded African American and millennial outreach, data, tech, research, and organizers on the ground -- which was almost certainly the difference between winning and losing in that race.

All in all, we're in good shape right now, Derek. Our debt is at its lowest point for an off-cycle year since 2009 and we have more cash on hand than we had in 2005 and 2007 -- years that both preceded major Democratic wave elections. We're in especially good shape when you consider that we're fundraising the right way, from the ground up.

You can't say that the Republicans are raising money the right way, not even a little bit. Plus the GOP's one real legislative achievement last year was slashing taxes for the wealthy in a system rigged to favor the 1%. So there's nothing surprising about their ability to attract big donations from the likes of the Koch brothers to pad their war chest -- money is #1 on their list of values and it shows.

**Because of all that, the GOP will outraise us. But that's OK, because even though we're being outspent, Democrats are winning on the most important scoreboard in politics -- we're winning elections.**

Since Trump took office, we've won over 100 local and state elections and flipped 34 state legislative seats from red to blue. We've picked up seats in deep red districts in Georgia, Oklahoma, and Iowa. And we've won critical races in Virginia, New Jersey, and Alabama, where Doug Jones just became the state's first Democratic senator to be elected in 25 years.

We're winning because Democrats are fighting for working families, for people struggling to make it into the middle class, and for people who didn't inherit their wealth. And we're winning because we're investing in organizing in every ZIP code, building up our state parties, and getting people registered to vote.

**We can win while the Republicans are outspending us. We've already proven that. But we can't take anything for granted, Derek, and we're not. Your support today will ensure Democrats have the resources to compete and win.**

**[Our work is just getting started. Chip in $3 right now and together we'll elect Democrats nationwide this year.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223fe/1181813120/VEsFBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-the-dnc?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=31+-+Our+work+is+just+getting+started+Chip+in&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")**

On Tuesday, November 6th, votes will be counted across the country in an election that could be historic for the Democratic Party.

Right now it's up to us to keep up the fight. Thanks for being a part of it.

Henry

Henry R. Muñoz III
National Finance Chairman
Democratic National Committee

P.S. -- **Here's the bottom line: The leader of the Republican Party is a billionaire fighting for billionaires.** Of course they're going to have more money than we do. But we can stop them if we come together to take them on. **[The time to take action is now -- chip in $3 today.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223ff/1181813120/VEsFBw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-the-dnc?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=32+-+The+time+to+take+action+is+now++chip+in+&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1")**

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223fc/1181813120/VEsFBg/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=33+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223fd/1181813120/VEsFAQ/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=34+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223fa/1181813120/VEsFAA/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=35+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052212c/4067b0a/548261cf4/7e5223fb/1181813120/VEsFAw/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=36+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180207_hm_s1&source=em_20180207_hm_s1&refcode=em_20180207_hm_s1"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Ben Jealous
subject: Time to dream again
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friend -
Traveling across this great state, I've met folks from Western Maryland, to the Eastern shore, to Baltimore City, who all want the same things: good paying jobs, great teachers in every classroom, affordable healthcare, and the opportunity to grow their businesses and earn a decent wage.
Like me, these Marylanders know what our state is capable of when we aren't afraid to dream again - when we challenge ourselves and our state's leadership to act boldly and swiftly to build a government that is more accountable and responsive to the needs of its people.
That is why at this moment, we must find the courage to do what we know is right for ourselves, our neighbors, and our children's future - and it's why I'm running to be your next governor of Maryland.
**[Donate $10 to say you're with me in this fight.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a70b/7ab0e2c4/7f4c317e/349a237c/125675347/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj_welcome?refcode=OHQG17A")**
Today we find ourselves at a precarious moment in time - where day after day our progress is undermined by a federal government controlled by far right-wing extremists and a governor who has become more like the cowardly lion in the Wizard of Oz - all talk, no courage. He has stood silent while these extremists repeatedly put the wealth of a few above our neighbor's lives and our children's future.
Since President Trump took office, Governor Hogan has repeatedly bowed and submitted to the agendas of his party's greatest extremists including Education Secretary Betsy DeVos, EPA Chief Scott Pruitt, and Attorney General Jeff Sessions.
As president and CEO of the NAACP, I helped lead successful efforts in Maryland to pass the DREAM Act, achieve marriage equality, and abolish the death penalty. Simultaneously we also helped expand voting rights and passed sensible gun safety reform.
There is no shortage of solutions, just a shortage of leaders with the courage to implement them. We can tap into the ideals that make us great. We can grow our state, create opportunity, invest in our future, and leave a better state for our children.
That means protecting the legacy of President Obama, bringing Bernie's dreams into reality, and building a future for Maryland that harnesses all our hopes and dreams.
It will not be easy, but no great undertaking ever is. I don't fear the challenge. I am inspired by it. I welcome the chance to build a better future and to leave behind a better world for all who follow.
[**Work with me to create a more united Maryland. Chip in $10 to our campaign today.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a70b/7ab0e2c4/7f4c317e/349a237c/125675347/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj_welcome?refcode=OHQG17A")
Onward,
Ben

**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a70b/7ab0e2c4/7f4c317e/349a237c/125675347/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj_welcome?refcode=OHQG17A")**

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER

Email is one of the most critical tools for our campaign, but you can let us know if you would like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a70b/7ab0e2c4/7f4c317e/349a237e/125675347/VEsO/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=emfooter") going forward. name: Dan Lipinski
subject: the fight for working families is still on
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Lipinski for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/3eS34_O-zcSVTTczXQpJH5_MGGvIQwHrDx36Gd4V2nw=/jZw9dLOgmoTXgch1UVIslXz9cUC4UbqSRUdzLw_o5Lo=)

Peter,

Growing up on the Southwest Side of Chicago, I learned the values of hard work and service to others, as well as the importance of using common sense to lead.

As the Representative from Illinois' 3rd District, I've used these principles -- along with the problem-solving skills I learned as an engineer -- to address critical issues that middle-class families in our district are facing every day.

On March 20th, Democrats in our district will head to the polls to vote in our primary election. I'm committed to fighting in Congress for Medicare, Social Security, better jobs, and helping our veterans and seniors.

[**Will you join Team Lipinski and help me double down on the progress we 've made?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/3eS34_O-zcSVTTczXQpJH5_MGGvIQwHrDx36Gd4V2nw=/LrBCmBEUuyPfZrh51jQ0dugxHbbrPFiu0zxAEinNaQA=?recipientId=3sRleyVCGuRfCtuAizxFV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)

No one understands this district, its people, or their concerns the way I do. I take immense pride in the work my staff and I do to help constituents and local communities here in the 3rd. My commitment to the working families here at home is why they've chosen me as their representative in Congress seven times.

[**But there 's still so much to be done. I'm more determined than ever to see that work through. If you're with us, click here to join Team Lipinski >>>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/3eS34_O-zcSVTTczXQpJH5_MGGvIQwHrDx36Gd4V2nw=/LrBCmBEUuyPfZrh51jQ0dugxHbbrPFiu0zxAEinNaQA=?recipientId=3sRleyVCGuRfCtuAizxFV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)

In Congress, I bring the priorities of Illinois working families to the national debates facing our country. I bring people together to solve problems like middle class job creation, fixing our transportation systems, making health care more affordable, helping small businesses, investing in public education, promoting American innovation, and honoring veterans and seniors.

That's why the Illinoisans of this district chose me as their representative in Congress, and in 2018, I plan to keep fighting for them against Trump's right-wing agenda.

[**Click here to join our team as we head into this critical election year; we need to know that we have your support in the tough fights ahead >>>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/3eS34_O-zcSVTTczXQpJH5_MGGvIQwHrDx36Gd4V2nw=/LrBCmBEUuyPfZrh51jQ0dugxHbbrPFiu0zxAEinNaQA=?recipientId=3sRleyVCGuRfCtuAizxFV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)

2018 is our chance to reinforce the most important check on Trump, break through the status quo of Washington, and end the gridlock that hurts our nation.

[JOIN TEAM LIPINSKI >>](https://act.myngp.com/el/3eS34_O-zcSVTTczXQpJH5_MGGvIQwHrDx36Gd4V2nw=/LrBCmBEUuyPfZrh51jQ0dugxHbbrPFiu0zxAEinNaQA=?recipientId=3sRleyVCGuRfCtuAizxFV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)
It is an honor to serve as your representative in the U.S. Congress. I hope that I've earned your vote in the Democratic Primary on March 20, 2018.

\- Dan

I'm committed to fighting in Congress for Medicare, Social Security, better jobs, and helping our veterans and seniors. No one understands this district, its people, or their concerns the way I do. I take immense pride in the work my staff and I do to help constituents and local communities here in the 3rd. My commitment to the working families here at home is why they've chosen me as their representative in Congress seven times. In Congress, I bring the priorities of Illinois working families to the national debates facing our country. I bring people together to solve problems like middle class job creation, fixing our transportation systems, making health care more affordable, helping small businesses, investing in public education, promoting American innovation, and honoring veterans and seniors. That's why the Illinoisans of this district chose me as their representative in Congress, and in 2018, I plan to keep fighting for them against Trump's right-wing agenda. 2018 is our chance to reinforce the most important check on Trump, break through the status quo of Washington, and end the gridlock that hurts our nation. It is an honor to serve as your representative in the U.S. Congress. I hope that I've earned your vote in the Democratic Primary on March 20, 2018. \- Dan Thanks for your support of our campaign. As I’ve traveled this last month and talked to voters from across our great state, I’ve been met with one amazing constant: Wisconsinites are seeing..

Friend -

As I’ve traveled this last month and talked to voters from across our great state, I’ve been met with one amazing constant: Wisconsinites are seeing more money in their paychecks because of tax reform.

For example, Steve from Hartland is now saving $40 per paycheck. That’s about $1,040 per year which he’s using to help pay down his student loans.

And Mark is a machinist from Lyndon Station. Thanks to tax reform, Mark can keep an extra $50 of his hard-earned money a month. For him and his wife Katie, that help will pay for diapers for their one-year-old.

These are not crumbs.

Friend, you and I both know that it is all due to Donald Trump and a conservative senate.

Unfortunately, the Senate only has a one seat Republican advantage, and that could easily slip away. That is why we must take Wisconsin back.

Thankfully, you can do something about. We can turn Wisconsin red again, but to do that we must defeat Tammy Baldwin.

I know I can do that, but I need your support, which is why [**I’m asking you to chip in $25 today.**](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=244da11ebbc04b94a89ef039b640357d&_e=ODDkCJm2mp4oFUY_5Gpt0tmhOWktpIKvcHuwJcw7nUU1rCPzcLzmbtkaAONCbrtSNY2BdaLY56l03BpubFixDnEw5mIGTHUxlx5core9gGN8lsbxLg6tySaqIDoQ5wDSOD3MYMahJjAZ6RaWeV994aWsTmDG4zHUOtrtjM9AuN67fNz3DeYOPO2nli4JHTTy7D3yPt14uOSJQjTEtVUmkAR-RjJObfdQekjh2uN9-_neFZDJcJ_Fw1l8n3g5zpXyFmYskb_89UcFZ5r_5D_Bvt4N42oFjONK51coOyWb8HvYpC9QQk8-uHOvtBChmTAnumhwxfXzkTl9elsUAEZ1pJQhnddbIylhR2QdnTSpdImPhEeSLNbAfDr0irrX1tiSq6IfDDDkZfwxTuKDYB0xsOamwfrAgslzfapKMuBdwkRiG9nThay3XMJtPLpTWzNe)

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate
body:

Friends: It's been exactly one year since Betsy DeVos was confirmed as Education Secretary, but already she's done unbelievable damage.

She tried to slash more than $9 _billion_ from the federal education budget by gutting children's mental health services and anti-bullying initiatives.

She rolled back key Title IX protections for survivors of sexual assault on college campuses, making it harder for those who have been harmed to feel safe or seek justice.

And she reversed rules put in place to protect the civil rights of disabled and transgender students.

Our students deserve so much better, and **I'm going to keep fighting against the backward agendas of the Trump administration and Betsy DeVos. body:

---
| We heard a lot of misleading and out-right false statements at Trump's State of the Union address. We heard his game plan for immigration and infrastructure. You know what he didn't mention once? **Women.** I suppose issues like reproductive care or equal pay don't concern the President of the United States. Trump has to start taking the issues that affect women seriously. [_**Let Trump know that you stand ready to hold his administration accountable for attacking women's rights.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f83/622b7b5b/19514f92/307bb676/3685707181/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180207") The very first bill President Barack Obama signed into law after taking office was the Lilly Ledbetter Fair Pay Act. President Trump didn't even mention women in the biggest policy address he's given thus far. If that doesn't tell you how low women's issues are on the list of priorities under this administration, I don't know what will. But I can tell you this: **I will stand up to this President every time he attacks policies affecting women.** I have a reputation for calling out Trump and Republicans for their failures. When it comes to women's rights, I fully intend to live up to that reputation. [_**Join me.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f83/622b7b5b/19514f92/307bb676/3685707181/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180207") [_**Add your name and let Trump know you're ready to hold him accountable.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f83/622b7b5b/19514f92/307bb676/3685707181/VEsF/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180207") Thank you. name: Ben Jealous
subject: "Some of them just should have probably gotten off the couch"
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
Since Donald Trump called for an end the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program back in September, Republicans have created delay after delay to develop a lasting solution for the 690,000 undocumented children depending on it.
But yesterday, White House chief of staff, John Kelly, showed his cards and claimed DACA-eligible DREAMers who didn't sign up for the program were "**too lazy to get off their asses** " to seek protections.
**_Really?_**
These young people are anything but lazy. They're students, serving in our military, opening businesses, and working hard every day in the country that they call home.
**[Now we are mere hours away from yet another government shutdown with no long-term solution for Dreamers in sight. It's time for Republicans to end their excuses once and for all -- add your name right now and call on Congress to pass a clean Dream Act right away.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a70f/7ab0e2c4/7f4ed041/349a20fd/2173187149/VEsH/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/protect-daca?refcode=OHQ02078A")**

These young Dreamers are some of the best and brightest our country has to offer. To even qualify for DACA, enrollees must meet educational requirements, like being enrolled in high school or having a high school diploma or GED equivalent. In a Center for American Progress survey of DACA recipients, nearly all reported they had jobs, about 72 percent of respondents were pursuing higher education.
Trump has tried to pretend this is about the economy but the Center for American Progress estimated that the U.S. would lose about $460 billion in GDP over the next 10 years without DACA.
**The facts speak for themselves: those who take part in DACA have the right to live, work, and thrive here. And the Trump administration 's attempt to smear their character is unacceptable.**
**[Tell Donald Trump to reaffirm his commitment to Dreamers, and ask Congress to pass the bipartisan Dream Act.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a70f/7ab0e2c4/7f4ed041/349a20fd/2173187149/VEsF/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/protect-daca?refcode=OHQ02078A")**
Our great national experiment has thrived when we have chosen to embrace our diverse talents and perspectives, and it has failed when we have chosen to build walls between each other. These young people's skills, perspectives, and perseverance are what make our country great. We need to uphold our promise to protect them and return to them a semblance of the certainty that was stolen back in September.
Onward,
Ben

subject: McConnell Performance Survey (response requested)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Any way you slice it, Mitch McConnell's failed leadership is harmful to the American people and our democracy. |
---

| | | Peter,

Any way you slice it, Mitch McConnell's failed leadership is harmful to the American people and our democracy.

During his tenure in the Senate, he's:
1. **Spearheaded tax legislation that will ultimately raise taxes on the middle class,** spike the deficit by $1.4 trillion, and strip 13 million Americans of their health care.

2. **Fought to repeal the Affordable Care Act** and replace it with legislation that would raise premiums for the average Hoosier by $1,000 annually, take away health care from nearly 400,000 Hoosiers, and remove protections for people with pre-existing conditions.

3. **Celebrated _Citizens United_** \-- the Supreme Court decision that allowed billions of dollars of dark money to influence elections -- **as "the most free and open system we've had in modern times."**

It's clear that McConnell's extreme agenda is bad for America and bad for Hoosiers. Joe is fighting back against Mitch and his special interest allies, but he needs to hear from 1,200 folks by the end of the week:

**How would you grade Mitch McConnell 's leadership in the Senate?**

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: An issue of grave consequence that affects the lives of millions of Americans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
I am writing you about an issue of grave consequence that affects the lives of millions of Americans and greatly impacts our democracy
#
Sisters and Brothers -
I am writing you about an issue of grave consequence that affects the lives of millions of Americans and greatly impacts our democracy - namely the continued federal prohibition on marijuana and the need for reform of our criminal justice system.
As you know, a number of states (including my state of Vermont) have decriminalized or legalized the possession, use and sale of marijuana in recent years. Under the Obama Administration, the Justice Department took no action against these states or the people in those states. However, the Trump Administration has taken a very different stance with Attorney General Jeff Sessions threatening to prosecute. That would be a huge mistake and move us in exactly the wrong direction.
Here's why:
Millions of Americans have had their lives impacted by the federal prohibition on marijuana - arrests, convictions and even jail time. Even when people don't go to jail, the criminal record they receive makes it harder for them to find a job, get housing or go to college. Is this a widespread problem? It sure is. In 2016 alone, over half a million people were arrested for marijuana possession.
These harmful impacts are felt far more acutely in communities of color and poor communities because enforcement of marijuana laws is much stricter there than in more affluent, white communities. Incredibly, African Americans are four times more likely to be arrested for marijuana even though marijuana usage rates are basically the same across racial lines.
Of course, marijuana prohibition is part of a larger failed war on drugs that has led to the great national crisis of mass incarceration. Some 1.5 million people were arrested for a drug related offense in 2016 - over 80 percent of which were for possession alone. We need to stop criminalizing addiction. We need to stop criminalizing recreational marijuana use.
**The criminal justice system is not the answer to drug abuse.** Addiction is a health problem and we should start treating it that way. While communities all across the country lack adequate resources for treatment or prevention, we are spending approximately $50 billion a year on the war on drugs. That's absurd. We need to get our priorities right.
And that starts with making our voices heard:
**[Sign my petition if you agree it is long past time for the government to end its failed war on drugs and instead invest in programs that focus on treatment and prevention of drug abuse. This is an important issue that impacts almost everyone and we should all make our voices heard.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/war-on-drugs?source=em180207-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/war-on-drugs?source=em180207-full")**
This so-called war on drugs has led us to have over 2 million people in prison - disproportionately poor and from communities of color. Our incarceration rate is the highest in the world - higher even than authoritarian countries like China, Saudi Arabia, and Russia.
Further, what is not often discussed is how the war on drugs and mass incarceration is impacting the essence of our democracy. People with felony convictions cannot vote in many states. Today, for that reason alone, over 6 million Americans are denied access to the ballot.
Uneven enforcement and the fact that people of color receive longer sentences for the same offenses than white defendants means more felony convictions in those communities. And that means - surprise, surprise - fewer voters.
In other words, the war on drugs is robbing those minority and lower income communities of their political power. In Florida, Kentucky and Tennessee over 20 percent of voting age African Americans are disenfranchised because of felony convictions. It's not too hard to figure out what's going on here. The communities most impacted by these policies are systematically stripped of their ability in our democratic system to politically fight back.
Why hasn't something been done to fix this problem? You know the reason. The sad truth is that some politicians benefit from people not being able to vote. All too often these are the same politicians who are trying to disenfranchise voters in other ways, such as restrictive voter ID laws or extreme gerrymandering.
This has got to change.
We need the highest voter turnout in the world, not the highest incarceration rate. We need to provide treatment for people with substance abuse problems, not lock them up.
As a first step, we need to remove marijuana from Category 1 of the federal Controlled Substances Act where it is currently ranked alongside drugs like heroin. In fact, marijuana is classified more harshly than cocaine. That doesn't make any sense.
Let's have states decide the issue of marijuana for themselves like they do with alcohol. More and more states are moving in the direction of decriminalization. Let them make those decisions without federal interference.
Let's invest in the prevention and treatment of substance abuse.
Let's reform our criminal laws and take other steps to dismantle mass incarceration. Among other steps forward we need to ban private prisons and create new federal policing standards.
Let's restore the voting rights of all Americans.
If you share my goal of making these important reforms please sign this petition:
**[Sign my petition if you agree it is long past time for the government to end its failed war on drugs and instead invest in programs that focus on treatment and prevention of drug abuse. This is an important issue that impacts almost everyone and we should all make our voices heard.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/war-on-drugs?source=em180207-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/war-on-drugs?source=em180207-full")**
In Solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
